description_long: This tunnel structure features a long, cylindrical main passage intersected perpendicularly by a shorter, rectangular section, forming a cross-like shape when viewed from above. The main tunnel appears to be a smooth, round tube, likely constructed from a dark, metallic or concrete material, with open ends for entry and exit. The intersecting rectangular section is solid and robust, possibly serving as a support or access corridor. The exterior is uniformly dark, with a matte to semi-gloss finish, and no visible surface ornamentation or texturing. There are no visible doors, gates, or lighting fixtures at the entrances, and the tunnel appears to be open at both ends. The design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and structural integrity over decorative elements.
description: A cross-shaped, dark tunnel with a cylindrical main passage and a rectangular intersecting section, both open-ended and unadorned. The structure is robust, smooth, and utilitarian in appearance.
description_view_0: The tunnel is seen from the side, highlighting the cylindrical main passage intersected by a rectangular section at the center.
description_view_1: Viewed from the end, the circular opening of the main tunnel is visible, with the rectangular section extending horizontally on either side.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, showing the opposite side of the tunnel with the same cross-shaped intersection.
description_view_3: From the opposite end, the circular tunnel opening is again visible, with the rectangular section extending outward.
attribute_entrance_type: Circular open ends, constructed from reinforced concrete or metal, with no visible doors or gates.
attribute_surrounding_infrastructure: No visible surrounding infrastructure such as roads, tracks, or barriers directly adjacent to the tunnel entrances.
